calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-15-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-04-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Compel Initial And Further Responses To Judicial Counsel Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production And Cost Sanctions From Defendant Jookhouse Entertainment, Llc
Off Calendar Dec-04-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Albert L. Thuesen III, Esq. of Coit Law Group, Tel. 415.787.2648, for plaintiff and moving party;
Miriam O. Renzi, Esq. of Manning & Kass Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP, Tel. 415.400.0875, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: Defendant's counsel did not give timely notice to the Court that the matter will be contested; therefore, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. No arguments were allowed and the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CHIARA GARAY's Motion To Compel Initial And Further Responses To Judicial Counsel Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production And Cost Sanctions From Defendant Jookhouse Entertainment, LLC is GRANTED. The Defendant failed to provide timely, verified responses and failed to meet and confer in good faith. The Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $3,870.00.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

ASSIGN TO ARBITRATION CALENDAR LIST
Off Calendar Aug-10-2017 Set For Trial On Jun-18-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-27-2017 Assigned To Arbitration On Oct-16-17

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Aug-09-2017 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-18-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALBERT L. THUESEN III, TEL. NO. 1-415-787-COIT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Jookhouse Entertainment, LLC's motion to strike claim for punitive damages from complaint is granted without leave to amend. If and when plaintiff Chiara Garay obtains sufficient information to support alleging punitive damages liability against Jookhouse, Ms. Garay should file a motion seeking leave to amend to do so. The complaint does not adequately allege facts to support punitive damages liability against Jookhouse nor does it appear that, at the present time, it is reasonably possible for Ms. Garay to allege sufficient facts to support punitive damages liability against Jookhouse. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Paula Shi, CSR # 13358 =302/hk
